content: Portfolio update: AAPL 100 shares at 150.25, now 175.50. MSFT 50 shares at 300, now 310. GOOG 20 at 120, now 135.
Positions as of this morning — TSLA 30 at 185.00 now 210.45, NVDA 15 shares at 480 now 510, AMZN 10 at 145 now 155.
Holdings: META 25 shares at 220.00, currently 245.10. AMD 40 at 105 now 115. INTC 100 at 42 now 38.
Current portfolio: JPM 50 at 155 now 162, BAC 80 at 30 now 34, GS 15 at 380 now 395.
Tech positions: CRM 20 at 245 now 260, ORCL 40 at 115 now 122, ADBE 12 at 510 now 540.
Small-cap allocation: PATH 200 at 15 now 17, SOFI 300 at 8 now 9, RIVN 150 at 14 now 12.
Dividend holdings: T 100 shares at 18 now 20, VZ 80 at 38 now 40, KO 50 at 58 now 62.
Energy sector: XOM 60 at 105 now 112, CVX 40 at 155 now 160, COP 30 at 115 now 118.
We decided to use PostgreSQL because of the ACID guarantees and mature ecosystem, over SQLite and MongoDB.
Chose Rust for the high-throughput API layer because of its memory safety guarantees and predictable performance, over Go and C++.
Opted for Kafka as the message bus since we needed durable ordered streams with strong partitioning, over RabbitMQ and NATS.
Picked React for the frontend given team familiarity and component ecosystem, over Vue and Svelte.
Selected S3-compatible storage because of cost and durability, over local NFS and a managed EBS setup.
Went with Tailwind CSS for styling due to the utility-first mental model, over MUI and styled-components.
Settled on pytest for testing because of its fixture system and plugin ecosystem, over unittest and nose2.
Elected to run the worker in-process rather than as a sidecar because the cross-process IPC latency dominated real workloads. Alternatives considered were Celery and RQ.

Production incident on 2026-04-23. scope: compression-worker. description: stranded-running queue rows recovered via the v3.1.1 sweep mechanism.
Release on Apr 15 2026 — v3.1.0 tagged and pushed to the OSS repository, CI green on all platforms.
Deployment 2026/04/20 to production. scope: api-server. description: enabled request-ID middleware and Prometheus metrics.
Outage on 2026-04-10. scope: database. description: connection pool exhaustion during morning peak, resolved by pool size increase.
Team sync on 2026-04-22. description: quarterly planning review, agreed on Q3 priorities.
Milestone reached 2026-03-15. scope: product. description: 10,000 active users surpassed.
Handoff on April 18 2026 between the night shift and the morning team. scope: oncall. description: two open Sev-3 tickets passed over.
Announcement 2026-04-01 — new VP of Engineering joins, transitioning team structure over Q2.

To configure pgvector, first install the extension on your Postgres 15+ instance, then create a memories table with an embedding column typed vector(768). Index with HNSW for approximate nearest-neighbor search under 50ms.

For the OAuth integration: register a client in your IdP, set the redirect URI to /oauth/callback, configure the client ID and secret as environment variables, then restart the auth service. Test with a browser flow to /oauth/login.

Docker compose setup requires mounting the postgres data volume, exposing port 5002, and setting the MNEMOS_SECRET_KEY env var to at least 32 characters. Migrations run at startup via api/lifecycle.py.

GPU inference with llama-server: mount your GGUF model, set --n-gpu-layers 99 to offload everything, --ctx-size 32768 for long contexts, --flash-attn on for memory efficiency, and -ctk q8_0 -ctv q8_0 to quantize the KV cache.
Rate limiting is opt-in. Set RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=true and optionally MNEMOS_REDIS_URL for a shared backend. SlowAPI middleware reads the per-user key from the Authorization header.
Backup strategy: pg_dump the database daily, rsync the memories table to a secondary host, keep 7 days of rolling snapshots. Test restore quarterly by spinning up a recovery instance.

Observability stack setup: Prometheus scrapes /metrics, OpenTelemetry exporter sends traces to the OTLP collector on port 4318, structlog JSON output goes to stdout and is aggregated by the log forwarder.
Federation peer onboarding: create a user with role='federation', generate a Bearer API key, share the instance URL and key with the peer admin, then run the first pull via POST /v1/federation/pull.

To debug a slow query: enable pg_stat_statements, check the top 10 by total_exec_time, inspect EXPLAIN ANALYZE for the worst offender, add indexes or rewrite joins as needed. Latencies should drop below 50ms for hot paths.
DAG merge resolution: when two branches have diverged, the merge uses advisory locks on memory_branches.head_version_id to prevent races. Three-way merge with operator resolution is v3.4 work.
Bob deployed the v3.2 release last Thursday after the CI suite passed on both Python 3.11 and 3.12. The deploy target was the primary MNEMOS host. No rollback was required.
The team decided to postpone the mobile app launch until Q3 to prioritize stability improvements on the web platform. Initial customer feedback cited performance as the main concern.
During the retro, engineers noted that code reviews have been taking 3 days on average, up from 1 day last quarter. Proposal to implement automated PR assignment for better distribution.
API response times have improved 40% after the caching layer was added. The p95 latency dropped from 450ms to 280ms across all endpoints.
The security audit identified three medium-severity findings: missing rate limits on /admin/users, plaintext storage of federation peer tokens, and SSRF DNS-rebinding defense gap on webhook delivery.
Customer adoption metrics this week: 250 new signups, 85% retention at day-7, median session length of 12 minutes. Growth is steady but below target for the quarter.
